banner.title: Kling 3.0 AI Video Generator
banner.description: Kling 3.0 introduces an all-in-one multimodal generation framework with native audio, multi-shot storytelling, stronger subject consistency, and up to 15-second outputs. Pro-tier early access is rolling out now, with a broader release coming soon.

tabs.title: Text to Video
tabs.subTitle: Prompt
tabs.placeholder: Describe the video you want to generate...

tabs.title: Image to Video
tabs.subTitle: Image
tabs.tips: Upload JPG/PNG/WEBP images up to 10MB, minimum width/height of 300px.

details.title: Unified Multimodal Video Engine
details.description: Kling 3.0 unifies text-to-video, image-to-video, reference workflows, and editing operations into one native multimodal model. This architecture improves prompt understanding, creative control, and output stability in complex scenes.

details.title: Multi-Shot Storytelling in One Generation
details.description: Kling VIDEO 3.0 can interpret shot-by-shot intent from prompts and generate richer cinematic structure in a single run. It supports custom multi-shot narratives and smoother transitions without manual stitching.

details.title: Element Consistency with Multi-Reference Control
details.description: The model supports first frame + element references, plus stronger subject locking across camera movement and scene evolution. Characters, props, and environments stay more coherent from start to finish.

details.title: Native Audio with Character-Level Voice Targeting
details.description: Kling 3.0 upgrades native audio with clearer speaker assignment in multi-character scenes. It supports Chinese, English, Japanese, Korean, and Spanish, plus dialect and accent control for more realistic dialogue generation.

details.title: Native-Level Text Rendering in Video
details.description: Kling 3.0 improves text generation and preservation in-scene, helping maintain readable signage, labels, and branded copy. This is especially useful for ad creatives and product videos requiring clear typography.

details.title: Flexible 3-15s Duration for Richer Narratives
details.description: Compared with previous limits, Kling 3.0 extends maximum output duration to 15 seconds with flexible controls. Longer single-pass generations make continuous action and narrative pacing easier to produce.

details.title: Kling VIDEO 3.0 Capability Upgrade
details.description: The upgrade from VIDEO 2.6 to VIDEO 3.0 adds multi-shot control, stronger references, multilingual native audio, and longer duration support.

usageGuide.title: How to Use Kling 3.0
usageGuide.description: Create cinematic AI videos with Kling 3.0 in three quick steps

steps.title: Choose Kling 3.0
steps.description: Open Text to Video or Image to Video and select Kling 3.0 from the model list. Use text-only mode for fresh scenes or image mode for controlled animation.

steps.title: Set Prompt and Creative Controls
steps.description: Describe shots, camera intent, dialogue, and style. Add image references when needed for subject consistency, then set aspect ratio and duration based on your target output.

steps.title: Generate, Review, and Export
steps.description: Run generation, review motion/audio coherence, and export your final clip. Iterate with prompt refinements or references to improve shot sequencing and character consistency.

faq.name: faq
faq.label: FAQ
faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Learn more about Kling 3.0 and Kling VIDEO 3.0 Omni

items.title: What is Kling 3.0?
items.description: Kling 3.0 is a new generation AI video model series with a unified multimodal framework. It upgrades creative control, native audio, element consistency, and shot-level storytelling compared with earlier Kling versions.

items.title: What is the difference between VIDEO 3.0 and VIDEO 3.0 Omni?
items.description: VIDEO 3.0 is the upgraded core generation model for text/image workflows and stronger narrative control. VIDEO 3.0 Omni extends reference-based creation further with richer element workflows, including video character references and broader multimodal control.

items.title: Does Kling 3.0 support multi-shot generation?
items.description: Yes. Kling 3.0 introduces native multi-shot storytelling so you can describe multiple shots and transitions in one prompt, reducing manual editing and clip stitching.

items.title: Can Kling 3.0 generate native audio?
items.description: Yes. Kling 3.0 supports native audio output and improves character-level speaker control. It also expands multilingual support and handles more natural lip-sync behaviors in many scenes.

items.title: How long can videos be in Kling 3.0?
items.description: Kling 3.0 supports flexible output durations from 3 to 15 seconds in a single generation, enabling more complete narrative progression than previous shorter limits.

items.title: Can I keep character consistency across shots?
items.description: Yes. Kling 3.0 improves element consistency through reference inputs and upgraded subject control, helping maintain stable identities for characters and objects across scene changes.

items.title: Is Kling 3.0 available to everyone now?
items.description: Kling 3.0 is being rolled out gradually, with early access for selected users and Pro-tier subscribers in the official release note. Wider availability is expected in subsequent rollout phases.

items.title: What projects is Kling 3.0 best for?
items.description: Kling 3.0 is suited for short narrative videos, social content, multilingual dialogue scenes, ad creatives, and brand storytelling that need stronger shot control, continuity, and audio-visual coherence.
